name: Timpano Di Maccheroni Al Ragu Neapolitan Ragu
recipeInstructions:
1. Season pork all over with salt and pepper to taste and parsley.
2. Chop onions, garlic, and pancetta in meat grinder or food processor.
3. Cook the pork, ground pancetta mixture, and olive oil in a large casserole dish over very low heat until onions begin to color, about 1 hour.
4. Add red wine and cook, stirring occasionally. After about 2 hours, onions will be well-browned and most liquid will be evaporated.
5. Raise the heat to medium, add 2 to 3 tablespoons tomato paste, and cook, stirring constantly until the tomato paste mixes in and becomes dark brown. Repeat using 2 to 3 tablespoons at a time until all tomato paste is used.
6. Add tomato puree and 1/4 cup water, lower the heat, cover, and cook another two hours, adding water from time to time to keep the sauce from drying out.
7. When pork is tender enough that meat forks slide in easily, remove it from the sauce and set aside. Add sausage and continue cooking the sauce for one more hour until it becomes "dark, unctuous, shiny, and thick."
8. Combine flour and sugar in a large mixing bowl and cut in butter. Beat egg yolks and salt until light and add, little by little, until the flour mixture just holds together.
9. Divide into two parts, one slightly smaller than the other. Gather both into balls, flatten into discs, wrap tightly, and refrigerate for one hour.
10. Cover mushrooms with hot water and set aside for one hour.
11. Combine ground beef, egg, Parmigiano-Reggiano, parsley, salt, and pepper to taste with bread crumbs in a large bowl. Form into walnut-sized meatballs.
12. Melt butter in a small skillet over medium-high heat and briefly fry chicken liver and heart until cooked through, about five minutes. Remove and reserve the chicken parts.
13. Cook pancetta and minced onion over low heat until soft for about 10 minutes, then add white wine. Cook until the wine evaporates. After 10 minutes, add mushrooms and filtered soaking liquid, tomato paste, cooked chicken liver and heart, and meatballs. Simmer for 10 minutes, then set aside to cool.
14. Season pigeon breasts or chicken with salt and pepper to taste and fry in butter on both sides until brown, three to five minutes. Add finely chopped onion and white wine and cook, covered, until the wine evaporates.
15. Cool and cut meat into pieces. Roll out the larger pastry ball into a large circle and place it in a 9-inch springform pan. Gently push into corners to cover the bottom and sides of the pan and leave the remainder draped outside.
16. Cook perciatelli in rapidly boiling water until just tender, dress with 1 cup ragu, and 1/4 cup grated Parmigiano-Reggiano. Spread half of the perciatelli over the bottom of the pan. Scatter pieces of pigeon breast, peas, meat mixture, mozzarella, and hard-boiled eggs. Cover with another layer of ragu and Parmigiano-Reggiano.
17. Roll out the smaller pastry ball and drape it over the top of the pie. Trim to fit just inside the pan and fold excess from the bottom crust over the top. Crimp to seal, making sure edges are inside the springform rim.
18. Bake at 400 degrees until brown and firm, about 45 minutes. Set aside for 10 minutes before unmolding and serving. Makes 10 to 12 servings.
